page: Amazon Web Services i7i.2xlarge is a storage-optimized server powered by an Intel Xeon 8559C processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 8 vCPUs across 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 2.4 GHz. The processor includes a 320 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core.

For local storage, the i7i.2xlarge includes a bundled 1875 GB NVMe SSD, delivering high-speed local data access. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps. No dedicated GPU hardware is present on this instance.

In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates strong single-threaded performance, achieving a top-tier Geekbench single-core score of 2299.0 and a strong single-threaded gzip compression rate of 34.68 GB/sec. Memory performance is also strong, with a Passmark memory score of 2830.24 and uncached read bandwidth of 62.81 GB/sec. Multi-core and database operations, such as Redis SET operations at 12.29 million operations per second, align with average tier expectations. While small LLM text generation using a 135M model performs strongly at 303.72 tokens/sec, larger models like the 7B model show weaker performance at 7.17 tokens/sec due to the lack of GPU acceleration.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the i7i.2xlarge offers a balanced resource density for workloads requiring high-speed local NVMe storage combined with DDR5 memory. The inclusion of a large local SSD reduces the need for external block storage attachments for high-throughput tasks. This configuration is highly suited for database hosting, transactional processing, data caching, and localized file compression tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services i7i.2xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring an 8-vCPU Intel Xeon 8559C processor with x86_64 architecture, 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory, and a bundled 1875 GB NVMe SSD. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network speed of 5 Gbps. The server exhibits strong single-core performance, highlighted by top-tier Geekbench single-core and text processing scores, alongside strong single-threaded gzip compression and DDR5 memory bandwidth. Multi-core operations and database tasks perform at average levels, while LLM inference on larger models is limited by the absence of a GPU. Given its substantial local NVMe storage and high-speed memory, the i7i.2xlarge is well-suited for database hosting, caching layers, and storage-intensive transactional workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i7i.2xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 1875 GB NVMe SSD, designed for high-throughput database and storage workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i7i.2xlarge is a storage-optimized server with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 1875 GB NVMe SSD.
tagline: A storage-optimized platform combining high-speed DDR5 memory with a large local NVMe SSD for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8559C processor with 8 vCPUs and 320 MB L3 cache
64 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
Bundled 1875 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU and text processing performance
Optimized for databases, caching, and storage-intensive workloads
